title: anemone vitifolia var. tomentosa
intro: Anemone vitifolia var. tomentosa, commonly known as woolly grape anemone, is a plant species in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Anemone vitifolia var. tomentosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote wound healing, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
